evidence_text: The thinning rates observed in covered and uncovered ATS experiments were reported as 1-1.3 micron/min and 7-9 micron/min.

why_special: Protein size was reported as Stokes radius rather than molecular weight.
evidence_text: Alexa-Fluor labeled bovine serum albumin (fBSA:Stokes radius 3.5 nm)

field_value_json: "100 μm diameter region of interest; Gaussian 1/e2 distance a = 50 μm"
why_special: Important for interpreting tau and diffusion coefficient from FRAP.
evidence_text: Using the iris... a 100 μm diameter region of interest (bleach spot) was defined... bleaching falling off to 1/e2 at distance a = 50 μm.

field_value_json: "fraction of diffusible probe in dense phase at the beginning of FRAP study, excluding permanently trapped probe"
why_special: Theta could be mistaken for adsorption fraction but is a FRAP model parameter.
evidence_text: theta is the fraction of diffusible probe that is in the dense phase at the beginning of FRAP study... The term theta in the two phase model does not include permanently trapped probe.

field_value_json: "FRAP recovery may not approach unity at infinite time due to permanent trapping of probe."
why_special: Qualitative hydrogel-protein retention fact relevant to adsorption-like screening, though not quantified as uptake.
evidence_text: The recovery function, f(t), may not approach unity at infinite time due to 'permanent' trapping of probe.

why_special: Primary adsorption target is uranium rather than protein, but this explains why the hydrogel was designed and characterized.
evidence_text: PAO-CB showed the highest uranium adsorption capacity at pH 6.0 with the adsorption capacity of 868.01 mg g−1.

why_special: Relevant seawater-condition uranium adsorption benchmark for the same hydrogel.
evidence_text: At pH 8.0, PAO-CB still exhibited a high uranium adsorption capacity of 635.36 mg g−1.

field_value_json: "1469.79 mg g−1 in 128 ppm uranium solution; Langmuir calculated maximum 1466.98 mg g−1"
why_special: High-capacity non-protein adsorption result central to the paper.
evidence_text: PAO-CB showed the highest uranium adsorption capacity of 1469.79 mg g−1 in 128 ppm uranium solution. The adsorption isotherm fitted better with the Langmuir fitting model, which generated the maximum calculated uranium adsorption capacity of 1466.98 mg g−1.

field_value_json: "PAO-CB 8.56 mg g−1; PAO 5.92 mg g−1 after 56 days"
why_special: Natural seawater field-like extraction comparison and anti-fouling performance evidence.
evidence_text: PAO-CB realized a high uranium extraction capacity of 8.56 mg g−1, while PAO only showed a uranium extraction capacity of 5.92 mg g−1.

field_value_json: "18.91 MPa at break"
why_special: Mechanical property of hydrogel membrane important for deployment but not part of protein adsorption.
evidence_text: The PAO-CB exhibited a high tensile strength of 18.91 MPa at break.

why_special: Material property relevant to adsorption capacity.
evidence_text: PAO-CB membrane maintained a higher surface area of 1.56 m2 g−1, while the PAO membrane only exhibited a surface area of 0.68 m2 g−1.

field_name: BSA fouling decrease in uranium adsorption
field_value_json: "24.07% decrease at 5 mg mL−1 BSA"
why_special: Quantitative protein-fouling effect rather than direct protein adsorption capacity.
evidence_text: The addition of protein BSA to the concentration of 5 mg ml−1 only caused 24.07% decrease to the uranium adsorption capacity.

field_value_json: "The transparent PMMA chip was used directly as a waveguide for attenuated total reflection fluorescence readout."
why_special: Links hydrogel microarray format to in situ kinetic detection.
evidence_text: The whole chip consists of an optically transparent polymer, the chip itself can be directly used as a waveguide for optical readout using the ATR approach.

field_value_json: "Repeated printing over the same spot increased antigen fluorescence linearly without increasing spot area."
why_special: Hydrogel volume/probe loading is controlled geometrically by stacked printed layers.
evidence_text: The fluorescence intensity of the antigen bound in the immunoassay process increases linearly with the number of layers in the printing process.

field_value_json: "BSA and beta2-microglobulin gave similar readout signals despite approximately 30000-fold difference in protein concentrations."
why_special: Shows assay design can equalize signals across very different biomarker abundances.
evidence_text: The spots show similar signal intensities despite the differences in the protein concentrations by a factor of roughly 30.000.

field_value_json: "PDMAA surface-attached networks were described as intrinsically protein resistant and not absorbing protein from solution."
why_special: This is a qualitative anti-fouling adsorption claim central to interpretation of high signal-to-background.
evidence_text: PDMAA was chosen as a matrix polymer as it has been shown that surface-attached networks of the polymer are intrinsically protein resistant and do not absorb any protein from solution.

why_special: Hydrogel sample identity and durability depend on sulfhydryl substitution degree.
evidence_text: three types of SB-ST-D with different DS of sulfhydryl (DS of 6%, 12%, and 18%) were used to form SSD/PET, and they were termed as SSD6/PET, SSD12/PET, and SSD18/PET

why_special: Thin hydrogel coating thickness may affect adsorption and durability.
evidence_text: thickness ... increased from 18.5 ± 2.3 nm of SB-ST-D6 hydrogel coating to 28.4 ± 3.1 nm of SB-ST-D18 hydrogel coating

why_special: Hydrophilicity is linked by authors to anti-protein adsorption.
evidence_text: the WCA of PDA/PET decreased to 46.5° ± 1.8°... WCA decreased to 12.7° ± 1.5° of SSD12/PET from 15.3° ± 2.3° of SSD6/PET... SSD18/PET increased to 21.5° ± 1.7°

field_value_json: {"PET_APTT": "41 s", "PET_TT": "26 s", "PET_fabric_thrombus_weight": "28.9 ± 2.1 mg", "SSD18/PET_APTT": "143 s", "SSD18/PET_TT": "118 s", "SSD18/PET_fabric_thrombus_weight": "3.5 ± 0.7 mg"}
why_special: Blood-contacting performance is linked to anti-protein adsorption and platelet adhesion.
evidence_text: PET fabrics with a thrombus weight of 28.9 ± 2.1 mg, while that of SSD18/PET fabrics were only 3.5 ± 0.7 mg... APTT value of SSD18/PET... 143 s... TT... SSD18/PET was extended to 118 s

field_value_json: "SSDR/PET formed by conjugating vinyl-functionalized AGGGREDV peptide to SSD18/PET via thiol-ene click reaction"
why_special: Biofunctionalization changes cell selectivity while retaining hydrogel coating platform.
evidence_text: to conjugate REDV peptide on SSD18/PET (termed as SSDR/PET), the peptide sequence was designed to be AGGGREDV with a vinyl group in the N-terminus for the further reaction with the dihydrolipoic acid groups of the SSD/PET via thiol–ene click reaction

field_value_json: "Human lysozyme was used as a stimulus to disrupt aptamer-containing DNA crosslinks, and outcomes were FITC-dextran release or hydrogel degradation rather than lysozyme adsorption capacity."
why_special: Important for excluding or down-weighting this paper in adsorption datasets while retaining it for protein-responsive hydrogel mechanisms.
evidence_text: Human lysozyme will bind to the aptamer sequences on the DNA crosslinkers separating them and resulting in a lower crosslinking density in the alginate gels or alginate coatings.

field_value_json: "DNA-crosslinked alginate water content decreased from 91 +/- 2% to 68 +/- 5% after drying and reswelling in PBS."
why_special: Indicates additional DNA-alginate physical or ionic interactions that affect hydrogel structure.
evidence_text: The water content of DNA-crosslinked alginate decreasing from 91 +/- 2% to 68 +/- 5% (p < 0.001) when dried and reswollen in PBS.

field_value_json: "Average diameter 244 +/- 9 um over three batches; representative batch 254 +/- 17 um and range 50-500 um."
why_special: Particle size may affect protein access and release kinetics but was not tied to adsorption capacity.
evidence_text: The alginate microspheres synthesized by electrospraying had little batch-to-batch variation with an average diameter of 244 +/- 9 um over three batches.

field_value_json: "3 uM human lysozyme modulated release from hydrogels and microspheres."
why_special: Shows protein-responsive behavior at micromolar protein concentration.
evidence_text: The release of FITC-dextran from the DNA-crosslinked alginate gels and coated microspheres in this research was altered when human lysozyme was added to the release medium at low concentrations (3 uM, Figs. 5 and 7).

field_value_json: "Binding/release quantified indirectly from microgel volume changes rather than direct bound amount."
why_special: Adsorption capacity is inferred from swelling/deswelling and fixed-charge stoichiometry, not directly measured mass uptake.
evidence_text: It is a strength of the present method that interactions can be probed simply by monitoring microgel volume changes. However, direct measurement of the bound/released amount would avoid difficulty discriminating incomplete release and irreversible or very slow network changes.

field_value_json: "Cpep gel is the concentration of peptide in fully collapsed microgels, calculated as M Cp gel / Z."
why_special: This is a hydrogel-internal concentration proxy derived from charge stoichiometry.
evidence_text: Cpep gel is the concentration of peptide in the fully collapsed microgels, taken to be equal to M Cp gel / Z.

field_value_json: "No pramlintide detected in release medium over 72 h from 75 mg/mL turbid suspension."
why_special: A strong negative release outcome rather than fitted kinetic constant.
evidence_text: No pramlintide was detected in the release medium during the time of the experiment (72 h).

field_value_json: "Only 40% released after 95 h; k = 0.0024 h-1."
why_special: Explicit hydrogel matrix-embedded aggregated depot behavior relevant to sustained release.
evidence_text: The release profile revealed slow and incomplete release, with the released fraction reaching only 40% even after 95 h.

field_value_json: "Authors propose chondroitin sulfate, heparan sulfate, albumin, and fibronectin in future ECM models."
why_special: Design-space note for future protein-hydrogel adsorption studies.
evidence_text: The capacity of the method can be extended by employing microgels of other extracellular matrix polyelectrolytes, such as chondroitin sulfate and heparan sulfate, and including albumin and fibronectin.

field_value_json: "3.64 mmol/g dry gel"
why_special: Metal ion loading is a key affinity-site density variable that controls chelated hydrogel adsorption behavior.
evidence_text: Different amounts of Cu(II) and Co(II) ions (maximum 3.64mmol/g dry gel for Cu(II) and 1.72mmol/g dry gel for Co(II)) were loaded onto the gels.

field_value_json: "1.72 mmol/g dry gel"
why_special: Metal ion loading is a key affinity-site density variable that controls chelated hydrogel adsorption behavior.
evidence_text: Different amounts of Cu(II) and Co(II) ions (maximum 3.64mmol/g dry gel for Cu(II) and 1.72mmol/g dry gel for Co(II)) were loaded onto the gels.

field_value_json: {"Co(II)-PVIm": "pH 7.5", "Cu(II)-PVIm": "pH 7.5", "PVIm": "pH 7.0", "free_enzyme": "pH 6.5"}
why_special: Activity optimum shifts after immobilization and metal chelation; relevant biointerface response but not an adsorption capacity metric.
evidence_text: Maximum activity of immobilized glucose oxidase onto the PVIm, Cu(II) and Co(II)-chelated PVIm gels was investigated and pH dependence was observed to be at pH 6.5 for free enzyme, pH 7.0 for PVIm, pH 7.5 for Cu(II) and Co(II)-chelated PVIm gels, respectively.

field_value_json: "residual activity higher than 93% in the first 10 days"
why_special: Reports stability of immobilized enzyme rather than adsorption amount.
evidence_text: The stability of the immobilized enzyme is very high for all gels and the residual activity was higher than 93% in the first 10 days.

field_value_json: "about 90 min"
why_special: Common kinetic condition across gels and relevant for adsorption protocol interpretation.
evidence_text: Adsorption equilibrium were achieved gradually in about 90min under prevailing experimental conditions.

field_value_json: "less than 100 Å"
why_special: FRET result is interpreted as direct nanogel-Aβ interaction rather than conventional adsorption capacity.
evidence_text: This result suggests that the distance between FITC and TRITC was less than 100Å, indicating a direct interaction between CHPNH2 and Aβ at this concentration.

field_value_json: "120 nM or 100 ug/ml"
why_special: The same peak FRET condition is reported in molar and mass concentration units.
evidence_text: Both CHP and CHPNH2 reached peak FRET efficiency at 120nm or 100ug/ml.

field_value_json: "CHPNH2 had stronger low-dose FRET but greater cell toxicity than neutral CHP."
why_special: Material charge improved apparent binding at low concentration but worsened cell compatibility.
evidence_text: Neutral CHP, although less effective in cell-free FRET experiments, was more effective in preventing cytotoxicity than CHPNH2.

field_value_json: "Only homo-oligomeric Aβ species investigated."
why_special: Limits generalization to heterodimeric or heterotrimeric Aβ species.
evidence_text: We have only investigated homo-oligomeric Aβ species and further studies should be carried out with heterodimeric and hetero-trimeric Aβ species.

field_value_json: "317.5-637 mg LYS/g gel"
why_special: Single network cryogels loaded substantially more lysozyme than the paired IPN composites in the supplied table.
evidence_text: SNC loading values reported: 317.5, 534, 385, 637, 386.4, 344 mg LYS/g gel.

field_value_json: "43.5-157 mg LYS/g gel"
why_special: IPN formation lowered lysozyme loading relative to matched SNC precursors.
evidence_text: IPN loading values reported: 103.4, 95, 157, 84.6, 72, 43.5 mg LYS/g gel.

field_value_json: "Fickian diffusion"
why_special: The paper links release kinetics to Korsmeyer-Peppas n values below 0.5.
evidence_text: For all systems n r was lower than 0.5 and this indicates a Fickian diffusion of LYS from both SNCs and IPNs hydrogel composites.

field_value_json: "alpha = specific binding MIP / specific binding NIP"
why_special: The main quantitative adsorption metric is a normalized MIP:NIP selectivity ratio rather than direct adsorption capacity.
evidence_text: The molecular imprinting effect or imprinting efficiency throughout this work was characterised by the rebinding capacity exhibited by the protein-specific MIP in relation to the control NIP.

field_value_json: "specific-bound protein calculated by subtracting recovered non-specific protein in load and wash fractions from initial protein additions"
why_special: Authors avoided relying solely on SDS:AcOH elution because elution may be incomplete.
evidence_text: An alternative approach is to calculate the specific-bound protein by subtracting the recovered non-specific protein in the load and wash fractions from the initial protein additions.

field_value_json: "90% previously reported efficiency"
why_special: Incomplete elution affects interpretation of recovered bound protein.
evidence_text: This heavily relies on the 10% SDS:AcOH being 100% efficient in removing/eluting protein; however only a 90% efficiency has been reported previously.

field_value_json: "approximately 50% of imprinted template recovered during initial conditioning washes"
why_special: Residual template retention may affect rebinding interpretation, but authors reported no protein in last wash fractions.
evidence_text: For all MIPs approximately 50% of the imprinted template was recovered and quantified using spectrophotometric analysis during the initial conditioning washes.

field_value_json: "MIPpolyAA recovered 1.5 mg after rebinding/elution from 3 mg added"
why_special: Indicates possible irreversible protein rebinding not captured by simple elution recovery.
evidence_text: The total amount of protein recovered in the MIPpolyAA (1.5mg) after rebinding and elution with 10% SDS:AcOH is less than the amount used for rebinding (3mg), suggests some irreversible rebinding of protein to the MIP.

why_special: Crosslinking density was held constant even though optimum density differs by protein size.
evidence_text: It should be noted that for consistency all hydrogels in this paper were produced using a standard 10% crosslinking density.

field_value_json: "EMb optimum 15%; BCat optimum 5%"
why_special: Reported control experiments explain lower selectivity for some protein-polymer combinations.
evidence_text: Control experiments have shown these densities to be optimum at 15% and 5% for EMb and BCat respectively.
